firstNightReminder: Dusk
otherNightReminder: Dusk
name: Dusk

firstNightReminder: Dawn
otherNightReminder: Dawn
name: Dawn

firstNightReminder: See Other Night Reminder
otherNightReminder: At the beginning of the night, let the Carnival Reveller point at 1 player (no traveller). If this is a Demon or Minion player, the Carnival Reveller turns  into another good character. Otherwise, the characters of both players swap immediately. Inform the Carnival Reveller about their new character which may act later tonight. Sleep. Then inform the other player about getting the carnival Reveller character.
name: Carnival Reveller
ability: Each night, you choose 1 player (no traveller) to swap *good* characters with. Else you become a good character.

firstNightReminder: See Other Night Reminder.
otherNightReminder: Wake the Demonologist. If a player got or lost the Demon character or if a player's Demon ability changed, since last dawn, show a "yes", else a "no".
name: Demonologist
ability: Each night, you learn whether a Demon ability or player changed yesterday or tonight.

firstNightReminder: See Other Night Reminder.
otherNightReminder: At night and day, count the number of players whose registered alignment has changed since last dawn. You may add a "+1" :reminder: to each of these players (if they don't have already one). At down, reset the count, remove any "+1" :reminder:s. Now, wake the Ponerologist. Show your count of players with changed alignment, the number of "+1" reminders.
name: Ponerologist
ability: Each night, you learn how many players changed their registered (player or character) alignment yesterday & tonight.

firstNightReminder: See Other Night Reminder.
otherNightReminder: Late at night, wake the Esoteric and show a number. This number corresponds to the number of *alive* players who registers as an alignment or character that does not match any of their foreground characters to any other player or game rule. E.g. neutral players count into it, evil Townsfolk, good Demons, but also a Recluse or a Spy.
name: Esoteric
ability: Each night, you learn how many *alive* players register their alignment or character unnaturally.

firstNightReminder: Let the Joker tell you what good character they want to be before night 1. Nothing else changes. They might invent a character which you will not declare. You might refuse their request if it creates or changes public information, can end the game quickly (like killing arbitrary players) or cannot be bluffed or balanced without changing anyone else. Rest: See Other Reminder.
otherNightReminder: If the Joker turned into a character whose token you don't have or might not be known to others, they register as the Joker.
name: Joker
ability: Wish *any* good character to become. (You might be prompted to choose differently.)

firstNightReminder: The Doppelgänger *might* get another character token at the start whose ability they already start having. This *might* be an in-play character as well.
otherNightReminder: Early at night, if the Doppelgänger killed a player with any ability or by nomination and execution, show them to have the Doppelgänger and the character that the killed player *registers* as. Don't forget to play the new ability tonight if applicable. If they get an evil character, this (& only this) evil character is poisoned. Any obtained character acts "on their first night" when the Doppelgänger get them.
name: Doppelgänger
ability: You get the character of the last player you killed. If it is an evil character, it is poisoned. (You might get another character at setup time.)

otherNightReminder: If the Quizmaster chose a player last day who gave a definitely (factually) wrong answer to their question, this player dies tonight. (They can refuse to answer.)
name: Quizmaster
ability: Each day, publicly choose ≤ 1 alive player to give an answer to your question. If factually wrong, they die tonight. (You might be a Fabled.)

name: Champion
ability: Once per game, publicly at day, choose 1 player: if they don't replicate what you show (3 tries), they are drunk for the next 2 cycles.

otherNightReminder: If the Hero would die tonight, they do not and the "sacrifize" :reminder: is added to them. On a later night, if the Hero has the "sacrifize" reminder, is not kill-attacked that night but *another* good player would die, the Hero dies instead.
name: Hero
ability: If you would die tonight, you die instead of *another* good player on a later night.

otherNightReminder: When the Darling is chosen by an evil player, add an "drunk 1" :reminder: to the player of the evil ability immediately. Next dusk, replace it with the "drunk 2" :reminder:. While "drunk X" is set, the player is drunk in the night and the day. It affects player abilities *retrospectively*. Next dusk, remove the reminder. It also happens if the Darling has died.
name: Darling
ability: Each evil player who chooses you via ability is drunk this & next cycle, even if you died.

name: Fighter
ability: Players who kill you are drunk the next 2 cycles.

firstNightReminder: Choose 1 Townsfolk player from the largest row of successive Townsfolk players & add the "know" :reminder: to the player. Show the Cartographer the player with that reminder.
name: Cartographer
ability: You start knowing one player of the longest row of Townsfolk players (ignoring Travellers).

firstNightReminder: Let the Lover choose 1 player (not themself). Add the "beloved" :reminder: to this player and inform the Lover about their alignment. Inform the player with "beloved" reminder that the Lover has chosen them. Rest: See Other Night Reminder.
otherNightReminder: At night or day, the Lover (even if dead) registers as the alignment of the player with "beloved" reminder. If the Lover would change alignment, the player with "beloved" reminder gets this alignment. When the Lover dies (even after being resurrected) or the player with "beloved" reminder dies, both players die together.
name: Lover
ability: On your 1st night, choose 1 player (not yourself) who gets to know you. You share the alignment & death with each other, even if dead.
attribution: character by Jura from Werworrias, slightly modified by me, image: https://de.freepik.com/vektoren-premium/silhouette-eines-paares-und-ein-rotes-herz_801364.htm

firstNightReminder: See Other NIght Reminder.
otherNightReminder: If the Aristocrat has no "used" reminder and ≥ 7 players are alive, wake the Aristocrat and allow them to choose 1 player. If they choose 1 player, add a "neutral" :reminder: to the Aristocrat and a "neutral" :reminder: to the pointed player. Also add the "used" :reminder: to the Aristocrat. If a player with "neutral" reminder changes their alignment, remove the "neutral" reminder. Players with "neutral" reminder have the neutral alignment (they register as opposite to the alignment of their actual character), see neutral alignment.
name: Aristocrat
ability: Once per game, when ≥ 7 players are alive, choose 1 *other* player. You & then this player get the neutral alignment. The event *might* be declared at dawn.

firstNightReminder: See Other Night Reminder.
otherNightReminder: At the end of the night, if the number of alive members of the Activist's team is greater than the number of alive members of the opposite team, the Activist changes to the opposite alignment. If this happens, inform the Activist about their new alignment. (Flip the character token in the Grimoire to indicate evil ailgnment.) The alignment change fails if no additional players of the target alignment are allowed.
name: Activist
ability: Each dawn, you change your alignment if the other alive players of your alignment are more than the alive players of the opposite alignment, even if dead.

otherNightReminder: If the Rookie nominates, add the "nominated" :reminder: to them. If the Rookie is not marked "nominated" and nominates the Demon, the Demon is executed (before or after the ballot) and dies (even if they could not). Add the "Demon" :reminder: to 1 alive player, they register as evil Demon. At night, inform the player who obtained the "Demon" reminder to be the new evil Demon.
name: Rookie
ability: You have 1 nomination per game. Any others fail. If you nominate the Demon, they are executed & assassinated & another alive player becomes the evil Demon.

otherNightReminder: Add the "rat" :reminder: to the player who kills the Big Shot (or failed at it). Tonight, any player with "rat" reminder dies (even if they could not). Then remove the "rat" :reminder:. (Optionally: there may be multiple "rat" reminders. You may choose to let 1 or more players die who voted for their deadly execution.)
name: Big Shot
ability: Good players who (try to) kill you are assassinated next night.

firstNightReminder: See Other Night Reminder.
otherNightReminder: Remove any "saved" :reminder:. Let the Frustrator point at one player and add the "saved" :reminder: to them in the Grimoire. At night or at day, if anyone would kill a player with the Frustrator's "saved" reminder, flip the "saved" reminder upside-down. A player with upside-down "saved" reminder is poisoned. When a player has the upside-down "saved" reminder while the Demon acts at night, ignore the Demon's attack targets and change the attack targets after the Demon chose.
name: Frustrator
ability: Each night, choose 1 player: if they are executed this cycle, they don't die & get poisoned next cycle instead & the story teller chooses Demon-kills next night.

firstNightReminder: See Other Night Reminder.
otherNightReminder: Even if the Amoralist is dead: When the game ends, the Amoralist inverts the final winning and losing result of every player who registers as alive towards the winning or ending rules. At day or night, a player who kills (or executes) the Demon turns into the new Demon. If a player would resurrect they don't.
name: Amoralist
ability: Even if you died: When the game ends, *alive* players win only if the opposite alignment would win. The Demon killer turns into the Demon. No resurrections.

firstNightReminder: See Other NIght Reminder.
otherNightReminder: When a player has died, wake them at night, show them "this is the Demon" and point to the Demon player. You *might* inform dead players about being informed by the Gnostic character (don't show the Gnostic player). At day or night, if a player would resurrect, they don't. The game also ends if a player calls for an end where 100% of alive players support it as long as the Gnostic is in the script. Dead players *might* be allowed to call for it. When the game ends, alive players win if evil would win, dead players win if good would win and Demons (evil and good) only win if evil wins.
name: Gnostic
ability: Even if you died: Demons & alive players win if evil wins. The others win if good wins & learn 1 demon player. No resurrections. The game ends if all alive players support it.

firstNightReminder: See Other Night Reminder.
otherNightReminder: For each rule that directly concerns ability text: read "good" as "evil". It does not affect words such as "Townsfolk" or "Minion" which can be both good and evil or which are neither of both.
name: Cynic
ability: "good" is interpreted as "evil" within all ability texts.

firstNightReminder: When you give Minion or Demon info (or a replacement info), you also show the evil character to each pointed player (if all pointed players have an evil character) or you show a list of evil characters that are in play. (In case of a Poppy Grower, you show them that a Populist is in play but not which player.) Rest: See Other Night Reminder.
otherNightReminder: For each good player at night (or day), whenever you would show an info signal (e.g. a thumb, a number, a player, a character…), you show it simultaneously with 1 additional false info signal. Use your own general sorting order between signals to show a set of signals always in the same way such that players cannot see which hand shows the false signal.
name: Populist
ability: Even if you died: Good players get each info in a pair with false info. They don't know which is true. Minion & Demon info additionally show character info.

name: Solipsist
ability: If the game ends, you win & all others lose – if you live – else you lose – if you are dead. You have every alignment + every character type, even if dead. [+2 Outsiders]

firstNightReminder: See Other Night Reminder
otherNightReminder: Once per night, you may wake the Identity Thief to point at a player with good character (you can reprompt them if they chose a wrong player). At day, you may allow the Identity Thief to tell you 1. Move the "victim" :reminder: to that player. You may refuse their choice attempt or choosing any specific player. You treat the Identity Thief as being the player with "victim" reminder (no effect, if the Identity Thief is their own victim). Thus, the Identity Thief shares the *same* abilities, properties and uses the same ability+player states for it but wakes directly after the victim. Interactions with the Idendity Thief interact with the victim instead.
name: Identity Thief
ability: Each night or day, you *might* choose a good character's player: you covertly register as being them until you choose again.

firstNightReminder: Choose a not-in-play Townsfolk ability for the Crimson Boy. Use the Character Token as a reminder. Show the Crimson Boy that character token before night 1. Play the Townsfolk ability tonight.
otherNightReminder: Play the Crimson Boy's Townsfolk ability at the proper time tonight or next day. If the Crimson Boy has no "misregistered" reminder AND any interaction of a good character uses the Crimson Boy's properties this cycle (= night+day), these properties are taken from the Crimson Boy's ability, exactly 1 time. If this happens, add the "misregistered" :reminder: to the Crimson Boy.
name: Crimson Boy
ability: You have a not-in-play Townsfolk ability. You register as it (towards exactly 1 good character) in the first cycle of being detected.

firstNightReminder: Replace 1 Townsfolk in the setup with 1 outsider.
otherNightReminder: Early at night, if new Minions or Demons have died since last time until now, inform the Brainformer about the characters that died. (The Brainformer would also learn if they died at night.)  Allow the Brainformer to choose 1 player. If it is a minion or demon (even if good), swap the characters of both players. No swap, if the other player is no minion or demon.
name: Brainformer
ability: If an evil character died (incl. yourself), you learn which & choose ≤ 1 player: you swap *evil* characters (if applicable). [+1 Outsider]

otherNightReminder: Right after the last evil killing ability, wake Epidemon to choose 1 player. If the player is dead by now, inform Epidemon about that (e.g. using a "dead" gesture). Let them confirm the choice or choose another player (in the same way). If Epidemon chooses themself, nothing changes. If another player is chosen, move (else add) the "evil" :reminder: to Epidemon and copy character AND alignment from the chosen player to the current Epidemon player (all of this in the Grimoire). Then turn the chosen player into the evil Epidemon. Inform all affected players (up to 3) of alignment and character changes. Once a player lost the Epidemon character, the die and (if it succeeds) the Demon-kill is triggered on their new ability in this night. Any player with "evil" reminder has the evil alignment (which – for the time being – masks the other alignment that they got from the new Epidemon player).
name: Epidemon
ability: Each night*, choose 1 player who becomes evil & swaps characters with you. When you lose Epidemon, you die & the host before you gets your old alignment.

firstNightReminder: After the Demon Info, let the Nimp point at 1 player (including themself). Add the "neutral" :reminder: to the Nimp, then a "neutral" :reminder: to the pointed player. You may only add a "neutral" reminder if < 3 other players are already neutral.
otherNightReminder: The Nimp points to a player. That player dies. At day or night, if the Nimp dies, the Nimp swaps characters with 1 player. Show the 'You are' card, then the new character to both players individually.
name: Nimp
ability: On night 1, choose 1 player to get neutral with you. Each night*, choose 1 player to kill. When you get neutral, you only swap characters with your killer (or 1 player) at death.

firstNightReminder: During the setup, replace 1 evil minion with a good outsider. Add 2 "recruiting" :reminder:s to the Mafia Boss in the Grimoire.
otherNightReminder: Let the Mafia Boss choose 1 player directly using a pointing or a gun gesture. If the Mafia Boss has ≥1 "recruiting" reminder, allow them to offer a recruitment to the pointed player by treating the Mafia Boss's gun gesture as an offer. Otherwise make no difference. If a gun gesture triggers a recruitment offer, remove a "recruiting" :reminder: from the Mafia Boss. Sleep. If a player gets a recruitment offer, wake them to give a thumbs up or down. Inform them about being chosen by the Mafia Boss. If they give thumbs up, they join the Mafia Boss's team. In this case, add a "poison" :reminder: to this player and a "poison" :reminder: to the nearest dead or alive *good* player (of any character type) in clockwise *or* counterclockwise direction – prefer the choice of Townsfolk players for balancing reasons. Pointed players – who are not recruited – die. Players with "poison" reminder are poisoned.
name: Mafia Boss
ability: Each night*, choose 1 player. Twice per game, you may let them choose to join your team & be poisoned with a good neighbour. If they don't, they die. [-1 Minion]
attribution: character by Jura from Werworrias, image: https://www.alamy.de/polizei-pistole-symbol-ubersicht-polizei-pistole-vektor-symbol-fur-web-design-auf-weissem-hintergrund-image236823247.html

firstNightReminder: Wake all *evil* Minions together, allow them to vote by pointing at 1 player (no Traveller) who they want to babysit Monstro. Move the "Demon" :reminder: (or Monstro token) to this player. A player with "Demon" reminder registers as the Demon. If there is no consent, decide by yourself. At day, if a dead player has no vote token anymore, their vote is still counted (ideally covertly) but *also (covertly) increases the number of ballot participants by 1*, raising the vote quota by ½ a vote!
otherNightReminder: See First Night Reminder. Also, 1 alive neighbour of the player with "Demon" reminder dies. You *may* wake the Demon player to choose which neighbour if you want but you can choose freely.
name: Monstro
ability: Each night, evil Minions choose 1 non-traveller who babysits you & "is the Demon". 1 alive neighbour dies each night*. Deads have unlimited votes. [+1 Minion]

firstNightReminder: Choose 1 townsfolk player as the Lleedh host. Add the "Poisoned" :reminder: to them. This player is chosen such that their poisoned ability is distinguishable from their healthy ability. Inform the Lleedh about this player as their host.
otherNightReminder: The player with Lleedh's "Poisoned" reminder has an ability which generally works abnormally by a rule, condition or just gets only false info. This must be a subset of a poisoned ability. Now, let the Lleedh choose 1 player. If unprotected, that player dies and gets the "Dead" :reminder:. If Lleedh is assassinated or slayed, the host dies with them.
name: Lleedh
ability: Each night*, choose 1 player to kill. You start knowing a Townsfolk: they are poisoned & their ability works abnormally - you die only if they die.

otherNightReminder: At dusk, remove any "checked" :reminder:s. The Fact Checker points at two players. Add the "checked" :reminder: to both. Any queried properties of players with "checked" reminder are correct, bypassing misregistration, even if queried by impaired abilities. If the Fact Checker is evil, the queried properties of both "checked" players are swapped additionally.
name: Fact Checker
ability: Each night*, choose 2 players who don't misregister (even to drunk/poisoned abilities) and – if you are evil – swap registrations, this cycle.

firstNightReminder: Add the "pause" :reminder: to the Psychotherapist in the Grimoire.
otherNightReminder: If the Psychotherapist has a "pause" reminder, remove the "pause" :reminder: and let them choose 1 player. Add a "client" :reminder: to this player. If the Psychotherapist is evil, rotate the "client" reminder upside down. If the Psychotherapist has no "pause" reminder, add a "pause" :reminder: to them in the Grimoire. A player with "client" reminder always registers as the aligmment, character and character type corresponding to an actual character that they have. If the "client" reminder is rotated, the player additionally registers as opposite alignment. The next time the player changes their properties, remove the "client" :reminder: from that player.
name: Psychotherapist
ability: Each even night, choose 1 player: they get their natural alignment (if you are good) or register as neutral (if you are evil).

firstNightReminder: See Other Night Reminder.
otherNightReminder: If the influencer has no "used" reminder, wake the influencer to point at a player. Add the "fan" :reminder: to this player and the "used" :reminder: to the influencer player in the Grimoire. Wake the player with "fan" reminder, inform them, that the "Influencer" chose them and which alignment the influencer has. If the game ends and if players with the Influencer's alignment win, the player with "fan" reminder would win as well (this is evaluated after all other winning conditions and abilities). At dusk, if a player has the "fan" reminder (from last night), remove the "fan" :reminder:.
name: Influencer
ability: Once per game, choose 1 player. They get to know you & *also win if you win* this cycle, even if they would not.

name: Commentator
ability: At day, the story teller partially reveals secrets by making an anonymized public comment on game events.

name: Spirit of Irony
ability: There can't be more than 1 extra evil player *with respect to actual alignment and in-play demon*.

name: Patron
ability: Any player who chooses ≥ 2 people or things doesn't need to only choose distinct people or things.

name: Supervisor
ability: Beginners don't die before day 3 and their death or uncovering does not lead to a strong disadvantage of their team. You can choose characters for them.

name: Duelist
ability: Each nominee for the execution duels their nominator (in a mini game). The loser is executed.

firstNightReminder: You must declare that you use the Loric Liar, even if you don't declare other Lorics or Fabled. The Liar must not apply to Homebrew rules and characters.
otherNightReminder: The Liar must not apply to Homebrew rules and characters.
name: Liar
ability: You may *say* false things about Blood on the Clocktower, the wiki or the game rules to deceive players. [No new or beginner players]
attribution: idea of Lins from Werworrias, image: https://www.freepik.com/premium-vector/top-quality-emoticon-liar-emoji-pinocchio-emoticon-with-long-nose-lying-yellow-face-yellow-face-emoji-popular-element_44311063.htm

name: Concealer
ability: You might conceal any other in-play Fabled and Lorics. You declare the total number of other Lorics plus Fabled.
attribution: idea of Lins from Werworrias, image: https://www.freepik.com/premium-vector/face-with-crossedout-mouth-large-size-yellow-emoji-smile_74352513.htm

firstNightReminder: Choose pairs of players who should receive a penalty (if they mutually communicate covertly) and add a "breakup ‹player 1›,‹player 2 ›" :reminder: for each in the Grimoire. Declare the pairs of players with "breakup" reminder. Also declare the penalty. Rest: See Other Night Reminder.
otherNightReminder: If you catch two players of a breakup reminder to *mutually* directly communicate covertly (privately) in any way, use the penalty for both.
name: Friend Breakup
ability: You declare pairs of players who are penalized for communicating (covertly) with each other.
attribution: invented by Lins from Werworrias, image: https://toppng.com/free-image/banner-royalty-free-best-friends-heart-clipart-best-friends-heart-PNG-free-PNG-Images_277876

name: Neutral (Alignment)
ability: By default winning conditions, the neutral alignment wins if & only if ≥ 1 of them is alive. ≤ 3 neutral players can be in play. If a demon gets neutral, they swap characters with another player. For anything else, neutral players register as good or evil players negating their natural alignment.

name: Natural (Properties)
ability: The true default properties associated to the player's current *actual* character (or any of the player's characters).

name: Ending the Game (Call)
ability: Once per execution, alive players may (dead players might) make a call for ending the game. If everyone is ready, a ballot starts. If ≥ 50% of the participants show support, the game ends.

name: Guessing the Demon (Call)
ability: Once per day, alive players may (dead players might) make a call for guessing 1 player as Demon. Preserves vote tokens. It's accepted if among participants (≥ 50% of all or 100% of good) AND ≥ 1 good show support. An accepted guess ends the day.

name: Hidden Traveller
ability: A player unknown to be a traveller who *might* register as non-Traveller & can be nominated (by alive players) and called to an exile (by all players) with a ballot matching the call, preserving vote tokens. Is always exiled when the ballot succeeded.
